context_units.diff_patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,19 @@
+from fastapi import APIRouter
+from sqlalchemy import select
+
+from infrastructure.db.session import get_session
+from infrastructure.db.models.review import ReviewORM
+
+router = APIRouter()
+
+
+@router.get("/reviews/{review_id}")
+async def get_review(review_id):
+    session = get_session()
+    result = await session.execute(
+        select(ReviewORM).where(ReviewORM.id == review_id)
+    )
+    review = result.scalar_one_or_none()
+    if review is None:
+        return {"error": "not found"}
+    return {"id": str(review.id), "summary": review.summary}

raw_diff_chunks.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,19 @@
+from fastapi import APIRouter
+from sqlalchemy import select
+
+from infrastructure.db.session import get_session
+from infrastructure.db.models.review import ReviewORM
+
+router = APIRouter()
+
+
+@router.get("/reviews/{review_id}")
+async def get_review(review_id):
+    session = get_session()
+    result = await session.execute(
+        select(ReviewORM).where(ReviewORM.id == review_id)
+    )
+    review = result.scalar_one_or_none()
+    if review is None:
+        return {"error": "not found"}
+    return {"id": str(review.id), "summary": review.summary}

expected_findings.title: API layer imports infrastructure directly
expected_findings.rationale: The route imports infrastructure DB session and ORM models, violating the dependency rule; the API layer must depend on application use cases, not infrastructure.

expected_findings.title: Database query inside route handler
expected_findings.rationale: The handler builds and executes a DB query directly; data access belongs in a repository called via a use case, keeping routes thin.
